title: The Exact Replica Threshold for Nonlinear Moments of Quantum States
abstract: Joint measurements on multiple copies of a quantum state provide access to nonlinear observables such as $\operatorname{tr}(ρ^t)$, but whether replica number marks a sharp information-theoretic resource boundary has remained unclear. For every fixed order $t\ge 3$, existing protocols show that $\lceil t/2\rceil$ replicas already suffice for polynomial-sample estimation of $\operatorname{tr}(ρ^t)$, yet it has remained open whether one fewer replica must necessarily incur a sample-complexity barrier growing with the dimension. We prove that this is indeed the case in the sample/copy-access model with replica-limited joint measurements: any protocol restricted to $\lceil t/2\rceil-1$ replicas requires dimension-growing sample complexity, while $\lceil t/2\rceil$ replicas suffice by prior work. Thus the exact replica threshold for fixed-order pure moments is $\lceil t/2\rceil$. Equivalently, for fixed-order pure moments, one additional coherent replica is not merely useful but marks the exact threshold between polynomial-sample estimation and a dimension-growing regime in the replica-limited model. We further show that the same threshold law extends to a broad family of observable-weighted moments $\operatorname{tr}(Oρ^t)$, including Pauli observables and other observables with bounded operator norm and macroscopic trace norm. Coherent replica number therefore acts as a genuinely discrete resource for nonlinear quantum-state estimation.
summary: This paper identifies a sharp, discrete resource boundary in quantum state estimation. It proves that for estimating nonlinear moments of order t, having ceil(t/2) replicas allows for polynomial-sample estimation, whereas having one fewer replica forces the required sample size to grow with the system dimension. This result establishes that the number of coherent replicas is a fundamental, qualitative resource for nonlinear quantum tasks.
main_problem: Determining whether the number of available quantum state replicas marks a sharp information-theoretic boundary for the estimation of nonlinear observables like tr(rho^t).
main_result: The paper proves that the exact replica threshold for fixed-order pure moments is ceil(t/2), where having fewer replicas leads to a dimension-growing sample complexity barrier.
method: The authors use a reduction to a spectrum-testing promise problem, constructing 'hard-pair' probability distributions using Newton's identities and applying Haar-integrated ensemble analysis and permutation inequalities.
model_or_system: A sample/copy-access model with replica-limited joint measurements applied to unknown d-dimensional quantum states.
key_observables: Pure nonlinear moments tr(rho^t) and observable-weighted moments tr(O*rho^t).
important_parameters: Replica number s, moment order t, Hilbert space dimension d, and number of measurement rounds T.
main_assumptions: The results focus on the replica-limited model and assume fixed-order moments as the dimension d goes to infinity.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the staircase-like nature of the exact replica threshold as a function of the moment order t.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of replica thresholds, establishes the lower bound via a reduction to spectrum testing and hard-pair construction, proves the upper bound using existing hybrid frameworks, and extends the threshold law to a broad family of weighted observables.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Exact replica threshold for nonlinear moments. The staircase ⌈t/2⌉indicates the exact threshold for the pure mo- ments tr(ρt) established here, together with its extension to the observable-weighted family tr(Oρt) considered in this work.
